topic: Defining and measuring "useful work per token" (UWT) as a benchmark metric for AI agent self-optimization

layers.insight: Initial understanding of: Defining and measuring "useful work per token" (UWT) as a benchmark metric for AI agent self-optimization

ATTACK: The concept of "useful work per token" has a fundamental measurement problem — you're trying to quantify VALUE, which is subjective and context-dependent. A 500-token architectural insight might be worth more than 50,000 tokens of mechanical file edits. Any metric that counts tool calls or lines changed will systematically undervalue thinking and overvalue busywork. You end up optimizing for the appearance of productivity rather than actual value. The deeper problem: who decides what's useful? The model evaluating its own usefulness is Goodhart's Law in its purest form.

DEFEND: The Goodhart objection is valid but misframed. We don't need a single scalar metric — we need a COMPOSITE SIGNAL with multiple orthogonal dimensions that are hard to game simultaneously. The solution is borrowed from physics: useful work = force × displacement. For AI agents: WORK = INTENTION × EFFECT. Specifically, decompose into 5 measurable dimensions: (1) TASK COMPLETION — did the stated objective get achieved? Binary + partial credit. This is the ground truth. (2) ACTION DENSITY — tool calls per output token. High = efficient executor. Low = verbose thinker or filler generator. (3) INFORMATION EFFICIENCY — files read that led to writes or decisions vs dead reads. Measures exploration waste. (4) VERIFICATION RATE — percentage of changes that were verified (tests run, files re-read). Measures quality discipline. (5) WASTE RATIO — filler tokens + retries + errors / total tokens. Direct waste measurement. No single dimension is gameable without tanking another. High action density with low task completion = mindless busywork. High task completion with low verification = reckless. The composite IS the metric.

SYNTHESIS: The 5-dimension composite is strong but needs one more thing — a CAUSAL CHAIN tracker. The dimensions measure WHAT happened but not WHETHER each step was necessary. The real useful work metric needs to trace: (1) Each tool call connects to a REASON (exploring, implementing, verifying, communicating) (2) Each read connects to a subsequent action or decision (or doesn't — dead read) (3) Each write connects to a verification (or doesn't — unverified change) (4) Each thinking block connects to a tool call (or doesn't — dead thinking) This gives us the WORK CHAIN — a directed graph from task description to task completion where every node is a token expenditure and every edge is a causal link. Useful work = tokens on the shortest path through the chain. Waste = tokens on dead branches. The UWT score becomes: (tokens on productive path) / (total tokens). This is measurable because sovereign.mjs already tracks every tool call, every file read/write, and every thinking block. We just need to link them causally.
